# Build Young

> A live, hands-on program for teenagers (ages 13–18) that teaches them to build economic value. Tagline: "Raising builders, not consumers." Financial literacy is the foundation; building value is the point. Students learn by living it — earning a simulated paycheck, investing, budgeting, managing credit, making big purchases, and launching a build of their own that other people would pay for.

## About
Build Young is an online program founded by Sunil Garg that teaches teens to build economic value two ways — getting money-savvy as the foundation, then growing it (putting money to work so it compounds) and, above all, creating something other people will pay for (you get ahead by adding value to other people's lives). It runs as a 12-week course (90-minute weekly sessions) plus 6 monthly check-ins, delivered live over Zoom. Each class, students earn a simulated paycheck and make real-world money decisions inside an interactive simulation. The program is financial education, not licensed financial advice; all money in the simulation is simulated.

## Who it's for
Teens ages 13 and up, in two tracks:
- Middle School cohort — $599
- High School cohort — $799 (includes a "Paying for College" unit on financial aid and student loans)

## What students learn
- Paychecks, taxes, and 401(k) basics
- Saving, investing, diversification, and compounding
- How macro forces (inflation, interest rates, recessions) move markets
- Big purchases: buying and financing a home and a car; good vs. bad debt
- Budgeting, credit and credit scores
- Building and protecting wealth (including alternatives like private equity) and avoiding scams
- Earning in an AI world — being a builder, not just a consumer

## Enrolling
Families can book a free 15-minute call with the founder before enrolling. A prorated refund is available through the end of Act 1 (first 3 weeks).

## Contact
- Email: team@build-young.com

- Terms: https://build-young.com/terms.html
